is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study genotypes P. falciparum isolates from infected pregnant and non-pregnant individuals in Senegal.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfcrt K76T mutation, i.e. codon 76 (a curated Pfcrt position).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Provides counts: controls 26 mutant/6 wild-type/17 mixed of 49; at delivery 50 mutant/3 wild-type/5 mixed of 58 peripheral; placental 57/2/8 of 67; during pregnancy 47/11/4 of 62.

sub_country_location.evidence: Conducted in the rural maternity hospital of Thiadiaye, Senegal — a named sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Women delivering between October 2001 and June 2002 — collection window under one year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human field/clinical isolates collected in Thiadiaye, Senegal.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study with PCR-RFLP genotyping of collected field isolates.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Women 'were part of a cohort study'; related publications (Jafari-Guemouri 2005; Tuikue Ndam 2004) draw on overlapping placental isolate collections from the same IRD Senegal unit.

supplement.evidence: All frequency counts are given in the main text; no supplementary material referenced.

assessment.summary: Primary study in Thiadiaye, Senegal (Oct 2001-Jun 2002) genotyping pfcrt K76T in 69 pregnant women (peripheral/placental) and 49 non-pregnant controls, reporting extractable mutant/wild-type/mixed counts at a named sub-national site within a <3y window.
